title: chrysolepis sempervirens
intro: Chrysolepis sempervirens, also known as the Santa Cruz cypress or paperbark cypress, is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree native to the Pacific coastal sage scrub and chaparral habitats of California and Oregon. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chrysolepis sempervirens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the bark, leaves, and twigs of the plant.
